as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="0J7BN10p2gEnQ1b3E8rs-95" parent="1" style="rounded=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;arcSize=8;absoluteArcSize=1;fillColor=#dae8fc;strokeColor=#6c8ebf;" value="backend server<div>IQ samples via API or SoapySDR</div><div>Audio out from IQ demodulator</div><div>or Audio out from analog audio in</div><div>CAT&nbsp; to/from FDM-DUO</div><div>CAT to/from IQ demodulator</div><div>Demodulator controls SoapySDR/API parameters from is CAT and translate parameters to CAT for client&nbsp;</div>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="230" width="260" x="750" y="230" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="0J7BN10p2gEnQ1b3E8rs-96" parent="1" style="text;html=1;whiteSpace=wrap;strokeColor=none;fillColor=none;align=center;verticalAlign=top;rounded=0;" value="<div style="text-align: justify;"><span style="background-color: transparent; color: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255));"><font face="Courier New" style="font-size: 8px;">IQ demodulator has its own CAT control similar to a real radio. Some CAT commands pertains to demodulator others to the radio itself. i.e. rf gain, agc, depending on the sdr hardware.</font></span></div><div style="text-align: justify;"><span style="background-color: transparent; color: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255));"><font face="Courier New" style="font-size: 8px;"><br></font></span></div><div style="text-align: justify;"><span style="background-color: transparent; color: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255));"><font face="Courier New" style="font-size: 8px;">User setup a onfiguration file for selection of SoapySDR or API IQ sampling, audio dongle or audio hat or none for portable radio.</font></span></div><div style="text-align: justify;"><span style="background-color: transparent; color: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255));"><font face="Courier New" style="font-size: 8px;">When in FDM-DUO mode, audio is from FDM-DUO usb audio port. The backend passtrough the audio, the CAT is used to display actual settings of radio, freq. bw, demodulation, rit, xit more can be added along the development, some CAT control for items not readily available on the radio for example items that need to be set via the menu of the radio.</font></span></div><div style="text-align: justify;"><span style="background-color: transparent; color: light-dark(rgb(0, 0, 0), rgb(255, 255, 255));"><font face="Courier New" style="font-size: 8px;"><br></font></span></div><div style="text-align: justify;"><font face="Courier New"><span style="font-size: 8px;">The FDM-DUO mode is unique, other sdr radio can not be used by themselves, so they alway use the backend IQ sampling, demodulator and controls from the backend, which was translated from CAT on the client side.</span></font></div><div style="text-align: justify;"><font face="Courier New"><span style="font-size: 8px;"><br></span></font></div><div style="text-align: justify;"><font face="Courier New"><span style="font-size: 8px;">Two modes for the backend FDM-DUO or SDR. FDM-DUO can use both, but not other hardware sdr.</span></font></div>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="310" width="310" x="240" y="490" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="0J7BN10p2gEnQ1b3E8rs-97" div.div:
backend server
IQ samples via API or SoapySDR
Audio out from IQ demodulator
or Audio out from analog audio in
CAT to/from FDM-DUO
CAT to/from IQ demodulator
Demodulator controls SoapySDR/API parameters from is CAT and translate parameters to CAT for client

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe flex-start; justify-content: unsafe center; width: 308px; height: 1px; padding-top: 409px; margin-left: 203px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: center; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: Helvetica; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;
text-align: justify;

span.font: IQ demodulator has its own CAT control similar to a real radio. Some CAT commands pertains to demodulator others to the radio itself. i.e. rf gain, agc, depending on the sdr hardware.

div.style: text-align: justify;
text-align: justify;

span.font: User setup a onfiguration file for selection of SoapySDR or API IQ sampling, audio dongle or audio hat or none for portable radio.

div.style: text-align: justify;

span.font: When in FDM-DUO mode, audio is from FDM-DUO usb audio port. The backend passtrough the audio, the CAT is used to display actual settings of radio, freq. bw, demodulation, rit, xit more can be added along the development, some CAT control for items not readily available on the radio for example items that need to be set via the menu of the radio.

div.style: text-align: justify;
text-align: justify;

font.span: The FDM-DUO mode is unique, other sdr radio can not be used by themselves, so they alway use the backend IQ sampling, demodulator and controls from the backend, which was translated from CAT on the client side.

div.style: text-align: justify;
text-align: justify;

font.span: Two modes for the backend FDM-DUO or SDR. FDM-DUO can use both, but not other hardware sdr.
